---
title: "CohortCharacteristics"
subtitle: "An R package to characterise cohorts"
format:
  revealjs: 
    theme: [simple, ../../styleSS25.scss]
    incremental: true   
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: true
    preview-links: auto
    margin: 0.07
    code-link: true
    code-line-numbers: false
    height: 900
    width: 1600
    footer: |
      <div style="position: relative; width: 100%;">
        <div style="text-align: center; font-weight: 500;">Real World Evidence Summer School 2026</div>
        <div style="position: fixed; top: 20px; right: 20px;"><a href="../index.qmd" style="text-decoration: none;">⬅ Back to Presentations</a></div>
      </div>
execute:
  echo: true
  eval: true
editor: source
---

## CohortCharacteristics

![](https://github.com/darwin-eu/CohortCharacteristics/blob/main/man/figures/logo.png?raw=true){fig-align="center"}

## Website

CohortCharacteristics is on CRAN:

. . .

```{r, eval = FALSE}
install.packages("CohortCharacteristics")
```

. . .

You can also install the development version from our GitHub repo:

. . .

```{r, eval = FALSE}
remotes::install_github("darwin-eu/CohortCharacteristics")
```

. . .

The package documentation and vignettes are available on the website: <https://darwin-eu.github.io/CohortCharacteristics/>

```{r, echo = FALSE, eval = TRUE}
options("width"=115)
library(DiagrammeR)
library(DiagrammeRsvg)
library(rsvg)
library(grid)
library(png)
library(duckdb)
library(CDMConnector)
library(CohortConstructor)
library(PatientProfiles)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(here)
library(CohortCharacteristics)
library(CodelistGenerator)

con <- dbConnect(drv = duckdb(dbdir = here("Databases", "GiBleed.duckdb")))

cdm <- cdmFromCon(
  con = con,
  cdmSchema = "main",
  writeSchema = "results",
  writePrefix = "cch_",
  cdmName = "GiBleed"
)
```

## Let's get started
Let's create some cohorts that we will use during the presentation:

```{r}
library(DiagrammeR)
library(DiagrammeRsvg)
library(rsvg)
library(grid)
library(png)

x <- c(list(anemia = 439777,
            rheumatoid_arthritis = 80809,
            osteoarthritis = 80180, 
            pneumonia = 255848),
       getDrugIngredientCodes(cdm, c("heparin", "clopidogrel"),
                              nameStyle = "{concept_name}")) |> 
  newCodelist()

cdm[["my_cohort"]] <- conceptCohort(cdm = cdm, 
                                    conceptSet = x,
                                    name = "my_cohort")
```

## Workflow

We have three types of functions:

-   **summarise**: these functions produce a standardised summary of a cohort. This output is called `<summarised_result>`.

-   **plot**: these functions produce plots from the `<summarised_result>` object generated by the *summarise* functions.

-   **table**: these functions produce tables from the `<summarised_result>` object generated by the *summarise* functions.

## Contents

-   [summariseCohortCount](https://darwin-eu.github.io/CohortCharacteristics/reference/summariseCohortCount.html)

-   [summariseCohortAttrition](https://darwin-eu.github.io/CohortCharacteristics/reference/summariseCohortAttrition.html)

-   [summariseCharacteristics](https://darwin-eu.github.io/CohortCharacteristics/reference/summariseCharacteristics.html)

-   [summariseLargeScaleCharacteristics](https://darwin-eu.github.io/CohortCharacteristics/reference/summariseLargeScaleCharacteristics.html)

-   [summariseCohortTiming](https://darwin-eu.github.io/CohortCharacteristics/articles/summarise_cohort_timing.html)

-   [summariseCohortOverlap](https://darwin-eu.github.io/CohortCharacteristics/articles/summarise_cohort_overlap.html)

## summariseCohortCount

. . .

Use `summariseCohortCount()` to get counts for your cohort. Pipe your cohort table into the function as shown below.

```{r, echo = T, eval = T}
results <- cdm$my_cohort |>
  summariseCohortCount()
```

. . .

It returns cohort counts in a `summarised_result` object.

```{r, echo = T, eval = T}
glimpse(results)
```

## summariseCohortCount

::: callout-note
Yesterday we saw the function `cohortCount()` to see the counts of a `<cohort_table>`.

```{r}
cohortCount(cdm$my_cohort)
```

The function `summariseCohortCount()` produces a similar result but in a standardised output (`<summarised_result>`).
:::

## tableCohortCount

. . .

To get a tidy table from the `<summarised_result>` object.

```{r, echo = T, eval = T}
tableCohortCount(result = results)
```

## plotCohortCount

. . .

Use `plotCohortCount()` to visualise cohort counts as a *ggplot2* object.

```{r, echo = T, eval = T}
results |>
  filter(variable_name == "Number records") |>
  plotCohortCount(colour = "cohort_name")
```

## plot functions in ggplot

The plotting functions return ggplot2 objects that you can edit later if you are familiar with the ggplot2 interface. For example, here we change the legend and the axis titles:

```{r}
p <- results |>
  filter(variable_name == "Number records") |>
  plotCohortCount(colour = "cohort_name")
p +
  labs(y = "N Records", fill = "Mock cohorts", colour = "Mock cohorts", title = "My Summer School Plot") +
  theme(legend.position = "top")
```

## summariseCohortAttrition

. . .

To summarise cohort attrition, pipe your cohort table into the function as shown below.

```{r, echo = T, eval = T}
results <- cdm$my_cohort |>
  summariseCohortAttrition()
```

. . .

It returns cohort attrition in a `summarised_result` object.

```{r, echo = T, eval = T}
glimpse(results)
```

## summariseCohortAttrition

::: callout-note
Yesterday we saw the function `attrition()` to see the attrition of a `<cohort_table>`.

```{r}
attrition(cdm$my_cohort)
```

The function `summariseCohortAttrition()` produces a similar result but in a standardised output (`<summarised_result>`).
:::

## tableCohortAttrition

. . .

To get a tidy table from the `<summarised_result>` object.

::: {style="max-height: 750px; overflow-y: auto;"}
```{r, echo = T, eval = T}
tableCohortAttrition(result = results)
```
:::

## plotCohortAttrition

. . .

You can create a cohort attrition flowchart like this:

::: {style="max-height: 750px; overflow-y: auto;"}
```{r, echo = T, eval = T}
plotCohortAttrition(result = results, type = "png")
```
:::

## summariseCharacteristics

. . .

The main functionality of the package is to extract cohort characteristics with `summariseCharacteristics()`.

```{r, echo = T, eval = T}
 results <- cdm$my_cohort |>
  summariseCharacteristics()
```

. . .

It returns cohort characteristics in a `<summarised_result>` object.

```{r, echo = T, eval = T}
glimpse(results)
```

## tableCharacteristics

. . .

To get a tidy table from the `<summarised_result>` object.

::: {style="max-height: 750px; overflow-y: auto;"}
```{r, echo = T, eval = T}
tableCharacteristics(result = results, header = "cohort_name")
```
:::

## Commonly used arguments for summariseCharacteristics

. . .

-   `strata`: creates stratified results.

-   `cohortId`: filters by cohort ID.

-   `ageGroup`: defines the age groups to summarise.

-   `tableIntersect`: count/flag/date/days

-   `cohortIntersect`: count/flag/date/days

-   `conceptIntersect`: count/flag/date/days

-   [link to documentation](https://darwin-eu.github.io/CohortCharacteristics/reference/summariseCharacteristics.html)

## ageGroup example

::: {style="max-height: 750px; overflow-y: auto;"}
```{r, echo = T, eval = F}
result <- summariseCharacteristics(cdm$my_cohort, ageGroup = list(c(0,10), c(11,18), c(19,150)))
tableCharacteristics(result = result, header = "cohort_name")
```
:::

## ageGroup example

::: {style="max-height: 750px; overflow-y: auto;"}
```{r, echo = F, eval = T}
result <- summariseCharacteristics(cdm$my_cohort, 
                                   ageGroup = list(c(0,10), c(11,18), c(19,150)))
tableCharacteristics(result = result, header = "cohort_name")
```
:::

## Strata example

::: {style="max-height: 750px; overflow-y: auto;"}
```{r, echo = T, eval = F}
result <- cdm$my_cohort |> 
  addAge(ageGroup = list(c(0,10),c(11,18), c(19,150))) |>
  summariseCharacteristics(cohortId = 1, 
                           strata = list("age_group"))
tableCharacteristics(result = result, header = "age_group")  
```
:::

## Strata example

::: {style="max-height: 750px; overflow-y: auto;"}
```{r, echo = F, eval = T}
result <- cdm$my_cohort |> 
  addAge(ageGroup = list(c(0,10),c(11,18), c(19,150))) |>
  summariseCharacteristics(cohortId = 1, strata = list("age_group"))
tableCharacteristics(result = result, header = "age_group")  
```
:::

## Intersection with another table

. . .

You can get count/flag/date/days summaries with other tables.

::: {style="max-height: 750px; overflow-y: auto;"}
```{r, echo = T, eval = F}
result <- cdm$my_cohort |>
  summariseCharacteristics(
    cohortId = "anemia",
    tableIntersectCount = list(
      "Number of visits prior year" = list(
        tableName = "visit_occurrence", 
        window = c(-365, 0)
      )
    ),
    cohortIntersectFlag = list(
      "Previous rheumatoid arthritis diagnosis" = list(
        targetCohortTable = "my_cohort",
        targetCohortId = "rheumatoid_arthritis", 
        window = c(-Inf, 0)
      )
    ),
    conceptIntersectDays = list(
      "Time since last X-ray" = list(
        conceptSet = list(pelvis_x_ray = 4145310L, clavicle_x_ray = 4058899L, chest_x_ray = 4163872L),
        window = c(-Inf, 0)
      ),
       "Time to immunotherapy" = list(
        conceptSet = list(immunotherapy = 4107731),
        window = c(0, Inf)
      )
    )
  )

tableCharacteristics(result = result, header = "cohort_name")
```
:::

## Intersection with another table

You can get count/flag/date/days summaries with other tables.

::: {style="max-height: 750px; overflow-y: auto;"}
```{r, echo = F, eval = T}
cdm$my_cohort |>
  summariseCharacteristics(
    tableIntersectCount = list(
      "Number of visits prior year" = list(
        tableName = "visit_occurrence", 
        window = c(-365, 0)
      )
    ),
    cohortIntersectFlag = list(
      "Previous rheumatoid arthritis diagnosis" = list(
        targetCohortTable = "my_cohort",
        targetCohortId = "rheumatoid_arthritis", 
        window = c(-Inf, 0)
      )
    ),
    conceptIntersectDays = list(
      "Time since last X-ray" = list(
        conceptSet = list(pelvis_x_ray = 4145310L, clavicle_x_ray = 4058899L, chest_x_ray = 4163872L),
        window = c(-Inf, 0)
      ),
      "Time to immunotherapy" = list(
        conceptSet = list(immunotherapy = 4107731),
        window = c(0, Inf)
      )
    )
  ) |>
  tableCharacteristics(header = "cohort_name")
```
:::

## Intersections

::: {.callout-note .nonincremental}
The arguments of the intersection functions match the *PatientProfiles* functions that we have just seen:

-   `tableIntersectFlag` -\> `PatientProfiles::addTableIntersectFlag()`
-   `tableIntersectCount` -\> `PatientProfiles::addTableIntersectCount()`
-   `tableIntersectDays` -\> `PatientProfiles::addTableIntersectDays()`
-   `tableIntersectDate` -\> `PatientProfiles::addTableIntersectDate()`
-   `conceptIntersectFlag` -\> `PatientProfiles::addConceptIntersectFlag()`
-   `conceptIntersectCount` -\> `PatientProfiles::addConceptIntersectCount()`
-   `conceptIntersectDays` -\> `PatientProfiles::addConceptIntersectDays()`
-   `conceptIntersectDate` -\> `PatientProfiles::addConceptIntersectDate()`
-   `cohortIntersectFlag` -\> `PatientProfiles::addCohortIntersectFlag()`
-   `cohortIntersectCount` -\> `PatientProfiles::addCohortIntersectCount()`
-   `cohortIntersectDays` -\> `PatientProfiles::addCohortIntersectDays()`
-   `cohortIntersectDate` -\> `PatientProfiles::addCohortIntersectDate()`
:::

## Your turn
```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE}
con <- dbConnect(drv = duckdb(dbdir = here("Databases", "delphi.duckdb")))

cdm <- cdmFromCon(
  con = con,
  cdmSchema = "main",
  writeSchema = "results",
  writePrefix = "cch_",
  cdmName = "delphi"
)

cdm[["my_cohort"]] <- conceptCohort(cdm = cdm, 
                                    conceptSet = x,
                                    name = "my_cohort")

```

Can you characterise the `osteoarthritis` cohort?

-   Start with only demographics
-   Add if they had any heparin prescription during the last 180 days (including index date).
-   Add if they had any pneumonia diagnosis last 2 years (excluding index date).

. . .


<details>

<summary>💡 Click to see solution</summary>

::: {style="max-height: 750px; overflow-y: auto;"}
```{r, eval = FALSE}
result <- summariseCharacteristics(
  cohort = cdm$my_cohort,
  cohortId = "osteoarthritis",
  cohortIntersectFlag = list(
      "Heparin prescriptions during the prior 180 days" = list(
        targetCohortTable = "my_cohort",
        targetCohortId = "heparin", 
        window = c(-180, 0)
      ),
      "Pneumonia diagnosis during the prior 2 years" = list(
        targetCohortTable = "my_cohort",
        targetCohortId = "pneumonia", 
        window = c(-730, -1)
      )
    )
)

tableCharacteristics(result = result, header = "cohort_name")
```
:::

</details>

::: {style="max-height: 750px; overflow-y: auto;"}
```{r, eval = TRUE, echo=FALSE}
result <- summariseCharacteristics(
  cohort = cdm$my_cohort,
  cohortId = "osteoarthritis",
  cohortIntersectFlag = list(
      "Heparin prescriptions during the prior 180 days" = list(
        targetCohortTable = "my_cohort",
        targetCohortId = "heparin", 
        window = c(-180, 0)
      ),
      "Pneumonia diagnosis during the prior 2 years" = list(
        targetCohortTable = "my_cohort",
        targetCohortId = "pneumonia", 
        window = c(-730, -1)
      )
    )
)

tableCharacteristics(result = result, header = "cohort_name")
```
:::

## plotCharacteristics()
. . .

You can plot a variable from the `summariseCharacteristics()` output using `plotCharacteristics()`.
```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE}
con <- dbConnect(drv = duckdb(dbdir = here("Databases", "GiBleed.duckdb")))

cdm <- cdmFromCon(
  con = con,
  cdmSchema = "main",
  writeSchema = "results",
  writePrefix = "cch_",
  cdmName = "GiBleed"
)

cdm[["my_cohort"]] <- conceptCohort(cdm = cdm, 
                                    conceptSet = x,
                                    name = "my_cohort")

```

```{r, echo = T, eval = T}
result <- cdm$my_cohort |>
  summariseCharacteristics() |>
  filter(variable_name == "Age")

plotCharacteristics(result = result, plotStyle = "boxplot", colour = "cohort_name")
```

## plotCharacteristics()

```{r, echo = T, eval = T}
result <- cdm$my_cohort |>
  summariseCharacteristics() |>
  filter(variable_name == "Age" & estimate_name == "median")

plotCharacteristics(result = result,
                    plotStyle = "barplot", 
                    facet = "cohort_name",
                    colour = "cdm_name")
```

## Your turn
```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE}
con <- dbConnect(drv = duckdb(dbdir = here("Databases", "delphi.duckdb")))

cdm <- cdmFromCon(
  con = con,
  cdmSchema = "main",
  writeSchema = "results",
  writePrefix = "cch_",
  cdmName = "delphi"
)

cdm[["my_cohort"]] <- conceptCohort(cdm = cdm, 
                                    conceptSet = x,
                                    name = "my_cohort")

```

Can you create a bar plot the percentage of sex in the `osteoarthritis` table?

<details>

<summary>💡 Click to see solution</summary>

```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=TRUE}
result <- cdm$my_cohort |>
  summariseCharacteristics(cohortId = "osteoarthritis") |>
  filter(variable_name == "Sex" & estimate_name == "percentage")

plotCharacteristics(result = result,
                    plotStyle = "barplot",
                    facet = "cohort_name",
                    colour = "variable_level")
```

</details>

::: {style="max-height: 750px; overflow-y: auto;"}
```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE}
result <- cdm$my_cohort |>
  summariseCharacteristics(cohortId = "osteoarthritis") |>
  filter(variable_name == "Sex" & estimate_name == "percentage")

plotCharacteristics(result = result,
                    plotStyle = "barplot",
                    facet = "cohort_name",
                    colour = "variable_level")
```
:::

```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE}
con <- dbConnect(drv = duckdb(dbdir = here("Databases", "GiBleed.duckdb")))

cdm <- cdmFromCon(
  con = con,
  cdmSchema = "main",
  writeSchema = "results",
  writePrefix = "cch_",
  cdmName = "GiBleed"
)

cdm[["my_cohort"]] <- conceptCohort(cdm = cdm, 
                                    conceptSet = x,
                                    name = "my_cohort")

```

## summariseLargeScaleCharacteristics

. . .

Sometimes we might want to summarise all clinical events for cohorts across different time windows. We can do this with `summariseLargeScaleCharacteristics()`.

```{r, echo = T, eval = T}
result <- cdm$my_cohort |>
  summariseLargeScaleCharacteristics(
    window = list(c(-Inf, -1), c(1, Inf)),
    eventInWindow = "condition_occurrence",
    minimumFrequency = 0.05
  )

glimpse(result)
```

## tableTopLargeScaleCharacteristics

. . .

`tableTopLargeScaleCharacteristics()` creates a tidy table of the most frequent concepts.

```{r, echo = T, eval = T}
tableTopLargeScaleCharacteristics(result = result, topConcepts = 5)
```

## tidy

You can create a tidy table that you can explore with the `tidy()` funciton:

```{r}
tidy(result)
```

## tidy

::: callout-note
The `tidy()` function works for any `<summarised_result>` object that we create.
:::

## summariseCohortOverlap

. . .

When creating multiple cohorts, we might be interested in the overlap of individuals between those cohorts. `summariseCohortOverlap()` does this for you.

```{r, echo = T, eval = T}
result <- cdm$my_cohort |>
  summariseCohortOverlap()

glimpse(result)
```

## tableCohortOverlap

. . .

You can also get a table with `tableCohortOverlap()`.

```{r, echo = T, eval = T}
tableCohortOverlap(result)
```

## plotCohortOverlap

. . .

You can also get a plot with `plotCohortOverlap()`.

```{r, echo = T, eval = T}
plotCohortOverlap(result = result, uniqueCombinations = TRUE)
```

## summariseCohortTiming

. . .

When creating multiple cohorts, we might be interested in the timing of entry between cohorts. `summariseCohortTiming()` does this for you.

```{r, echo = T, eval = T}
result <- cdm$my_cohort |>
  summariseCohortTiming(restrictToFirstEntry = TRUE)

glimpse(result)
```

## tableCohortTiming

. . .

You can also get a table with `tableCohortTiming()`.

```{r, echo = T, eval = T}
tableCohortTiming(result = result,
                  timeScale = "years", 
                  uniqueCombinations = FALSE)
```

## plotCohortTiming

. . .

You can also get a plot with `plotCohortTiming()`.

```{r, echo = T, eval = T}
plotCohortTiming(
  result = result,
  plotType = "boxplot",
  timeScale = "years",
  uniqueCombinations = FALSE
)
```

## plotCohortTiming

. . .

Or return a density plot.

```{r, echo = T, eval = T}
plotCohortTiming(
  result = result,
  plotType = "densityplot",
  timeScale = "years",
  uniqueCombinations = FALSE
)
```

## Your turn

```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE}
con <- dbConnect(drv = duckdb(dbdir = here("Databases", "delphi.duckdb")))

cdm <- cdmFromCon(
  con = con,
  cdmSchema = "main",
  writeSchema = "results",
  writePrefix = "cch_",
  cdmName = "delphi"
)

cdm[["my_cohort"]] <- conceptCohort(cdm = cdm, 
                                    conceptSet = x,
                                    name = "my_cohort")

```

Can you get the large-scale characteristics from the `drug_exposure` table for the `clopidogrel` cohort table using an anytime-prior time window (excluding index date)? Visualise the result showing the top 5 most frequent codes.

. . .

<details>

<summary>💡 Click to see solution</summary>

```{r, eval = FALSE, echo=TRUE}
result <- cdm$my_cohort |>
  summariseLargeScaleCharacteristics(
    cohortId = "clopidogrel",
    window = list(c(-Inf, -1)),
    episodeInWindow = "drug_exposure",
    minimumFrequency = 0.05
  )
tableTopLargeScaleCharacteristics(result = result, topConcepts = 5)
```

</details>

```{r, eval = TRUE, echo=FALSE}
result <- cdm$my_cohort |>
  summariseLargeScaleCharacteristics(
    cohortId = "clopidogrel",
    window = list(c(-Inf, -1)),
    episodeInWindow = "drug_exposure",
    minimumFrequency = 0.05
  )
tableTopLargeScaleCharacteristics(result = result, topConcepts = 5)
```

## CohortCharacteristics

::::: {style="display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between;"}
::: {style="flex: 1;"}
👉 [**Package website**](https://darwin-eu.github.io/CohortCharacteristics)\
👉 [**CRAN link**](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/CohortCharacteristics)\
👉 [**Manual**](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/CohortCharacteristics/CohortCharacteristics.pdf)

📧 <a href="mailto:marta.alcaldeherraiz@ndorms.ox.ac.uk">marta.alcaldeherraiz\@ndorms.ox.ac.uk</a>
:::

::: {style="flex: 1; text-align: center;"}
<img src="https://github.com/darwin-eu/CohortCharacteristics/blob/main/man/figures/logo.png?raw=true" width="600"/>
:::
:::::
